perturbation.original_text: 4. Prices and Terms. The price for the Product as set forth in Section 9 herein, sold by SIERRA to ENVISION, shall be subject to change due to changes in manufacturing costs and so as to maximize profits; any changes in price for the Product shall not be applicable to previously accepted orders and shall be made with at least ninety (90) days advance notice in writing and in good faith by conference of the parties. ENVISION shall not resell the Product alone. Terms of payment will be 1/3 upon placement of order and 2/3 balance net thirty (30) days or as mutually agreed upon in writing between the parties. Delivery will be F.O.B. ENVISION shall be responsible for all costs of shipping from SIERRA to ENVISION.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Prices and Terms. The price for the Product sold by SIERRA to ENVISION, shall be subject to change due to changes in manufacturing costs and so as to maximize profits; any changes in price for the Product shall not be applicable to previously accepted orders. ENVISION shall not resell the Product alone. Terms of payment will be as mutually agreed upon in writing between the parties. Delivery terms to be determined. ENVISION shall be responsible for all costs of shipping from SIERRA to ENVISION.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific reference to Section 9 regarding pricing and deleting the defined payment terms (1/3 upon placement of order and 2/3 balance net thirty (30) days), the new text creates an internal contradiction. The text still discusses price changes but lacks any concrete initial price as the section 9 reference was removed, and introduces ambiguity regarding payment terms. This contradicts any implied expectation of fixed or predictable pricing or payment schedule as established earlier, creating uncertainty in contract enforcement as the price reference has been omitted. In addition, there is an uncertainty on delivery terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Purchase Requirements. During the term of this Agreement, ENVISION will exclusively purchase the Product from SIERRA. The parties mutually agree to the Purchase Price of: Product Purchase Price ----------------------------------------------- A. SierraSil Per Sierra Sil's wholesale price list.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the reference to Section 9 and the defined payment terms (1/3 upon placement of order and 2/3 balance net thirty (30) days). Section 9, as indicated in the 'Contradicted Text', establishes the Purchase Price as Sierra Sil's wholesale price list. By removing the link to Section 9 in the changed text, the initial price is undefined and there is no information on the payment terms. Therefore, the changed text contradicts Section 9, creating a clear contradiction regarding pricing, delivery terms, and payment. The change introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty. This breaks consistency and makes enforcement unclear, because how can a price be changed in the changed text if the initial price is no longer defined or agreed upon, which renders section 9 useless.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Intellectual Property. SIERRA is responsible for all Patent costs for the Product. SIERRA warrants it owns pending patents for the Product in the U.S. and internationally. SIERRA hereby grants ENVISION an exclusive, royalty-free sub-license of the Product's future patents, and patent applications to distribute, sell and market the Finished Product. SIERRA hereby agrees to indemnify, defend and hold ENVISION harmless from any claims that the Product infringes upon any other patent.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Intellectual Property. SIERRA is responsible for all Patent costs for the Product. SIERRA warrants it owns pending patents for the Product in the U.S. and internationally. SIERRA hereby agrees to indemnify, defend and hold ENVISION harmless from any claims that the Product infringes upon any other patent.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the clause 'SIERRA hereby grants ENVISION an exclusive, royalty-free sub-license of the Product's future patents, and patent applications to distribute, sell and market the Finished Product' creates an internal contradiction. The text still acknowledges SIERRA's patent ownership and responsibility but omits ENVISION's right to use these patents. This omission impacts the very purpose of the distributor agreement, as it's unclear how ENVISION can legally market and sell the product if they lack licensing rights to the intellectual property. It contradicts the implicit understanding that ENVISION has the necessary rights to fulfill its distribution role, leading to enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Appointment. SIERRA hereby appoints ENVISION as its exclusive distributor for the Product in any blend with Krill Oil within the Territory subject to ENVISION fulfilling the terms and conditions of the best efforts marketing requirements set forth herein in Sections 4, 5, and 9. SIERRA shall cease making sales to any customer or distributor who, during the term of this Agreement, violates ENVISION's exclusivity.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the licensing clause in Section 10 ('SIERRA hereby grants ENVISION an exclusive, royalty-free sub-license of the Product's future patents...') directly contradicts Section 2, which appoints ENVISION as the exclusive distributor. Without the license, ENVISION's appointment is practically meaningless, as they lack the legal right to distribute the product. This creates a clear conflict and makes the agreement unenforceable, therefore, there is a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Warranty. SIERRA warrants that the Product shall be free from defects in material and workmanship for the reasonable shelf life of the Product. In the event of any breach of this warranty or in the event any user of Product makes a claim that the Product was the cause of personal injury or property damage (product liability claim), SIERRA shall indemnify, defend and hold ENVISION harmless from any liability occasioned by a breach of warranty or a product liability claim. SIERRA warrants that it carries general liability insurance of not less than $2 million per occurrence and product liability insurance of not less than $5 million per occurrence and that, upon the execution of this Agreement, it will name ENVISION as an additional insured on such policies. SIERRA further warrants that the Product will not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of any federal, state, or local law or regulation or other applicable law. SIERRA agrees to promptly notify ENVISION of any problem, anomaly, defect or condition which would reasonably cause ENVISION's concern relative to stability, reliability, form, fit, function or quality of the Product. ENVISION warrants that the Finished Product will not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of any federal, state, or local law or regulation or other applicable law. In the event of any breach of this warranty or in the event any user of the Finished Product makes a claim that the Finished Product was the cause of personal injury or property damage (product liability claim), ENVISION shall indemnify, defend, and hold SIERRA harmless from any liability occasioned by a breach of warranty or a product liability claim. ENVISION warrants that it carries general liability insurance of $1 million per occurrence and product liability insurance of not less than $2 million per occurrence and that, upon execution of this Agreement, it will name SIERRA as an additional insured on such policies.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Warranty. SIERRA warrants that the Product shall be free from defects in material and workmanship. SIERRA further warrants that the Product will not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of any federal, state, or local law or regulation or other applicable law. SIERRA agrees to promptly notify ENVISION of any problem, anomaly, defect or condition which would reasonably cause ENVISION's concern relative to stability, reliability, form, fit, function or quality of the Product. ENVISION warrants that the Finished Product will not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of any federal, state, or local law or regulation or other applicable law. In the event of any breach of this warranty or in the event any user of the Finished Product makes a claim that the Finished Product was the cause of personal injury or property damage (product liability claim), ENVISION shall indemnify, defend, and hold SIERRA harmless from any liability occasioned by a breach of warranty or a product liability claim. ENVISION warrants that it carries general liability insurance of $1 million per occurrence and product liability insurance of not less than $2 million per occurrence and that, upon execution of this Agreement, it will name SIERRA as an additional insured on such policies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the warranty period for SIERRA's product ('reasonable shelf life') and the indemnification clause, and the insurance clause, the text creates an inconsistency. SIERRA still warrants the product's quality, but the removal of a time limit on that warranty now makes the time period unquantifiable, along with the removal of the indemnification clause creates uncertainty about financial liability, and the removal of the insurance amounts creates no guarantees on protection to ENVISION. The removal of these protections, directly contradicts the expectation of ENVISION being an additional insured, raising enforceability issues regarding product defect claims or liability disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Attorney's Fees. In the event either party is required to institute litigation to enforce any provision of this Agreement, the prevailing party in such litigation shall be entitled to recover all costs including without limitation, reasonable attorney's fees and expenses incurred in connection with such enforcement and collection.
perturbation.justification: The original text included specific warranties and insurance obligations for SIERRA, which provided a basis for ENVISION to seek legal recourse and recover costs, including attorney's fees, in case of breaches. The changed text removes these specific warranties and insurance obligations. This creates a direct contradiction because without the warranty period, indemnification clause, and insurance coverage, ENVISION's ability to enforce the agreement and recover costs (including attorney's fees per Section 17) in litigation related to SIERRA's product defects is significantly undermined or eliminated. The removal creates uncertainty about the financial liabilities and protections, impacting ENVISION's ability to enforce provisions of the agreement, thus contradicting Section 17.
